records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.4139 - Commander of the Faithful (as) said regarding a hunted animal that was found with an arrow in it and was dead, but it was not known who killed it.
Imam (as) said: "Do not eat it."
And Imam (as) said: "Whoever wounds an animal with a weapon and mentions the name of Allah (swt), the Mighty and Majestic, upon it, and then the game remains for a night or two, and he finds it without a predator having eaten from it, and he knows that his weapon killed it, then he may eat from it, if Allah (swt) wills."
records.arabicText: 4139 - وَ قَالَ أَمِيرُ اَلْمُؤْمِنِينَ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ : فِي صَيْدٍ وُجِدَ فِيهِ سَهْمٌ وَ هُوَ مَيِّتٌ لاَ يُدْرَى مَنْ قَتَلَهُ فَقَالَ "لاَ تَطْعَمُوهُ " وَ قَالَ "مَنْ جَرَحَ بِسِلاَحٍ وَ ذَكَرَ اِسْمَ اَللَّهِ عَزَّ وَ جَلَّ ثُمَّ بَقِيَ اَلصَّيْدُ لَيْلَةً أَوْ لَيْلَتَيْنِ ثُمَّ وَجَدَهُ لَمْ يَأْكُلْ مِنْهُ سَبُعٌ وَ عَلِمَ أَنَّ سِلاَحَهُ قَتَلَهُ فَلْيَأْكُلْ مِنْهُ إِنْ شَاءَ اَللَّهُ".
records.thaqalaynSanad:
Hadith.4139 - Commander of the Faithful (as) said regarding a hunted animal that was found with an arrow in it and was dead, but it was not known who killed it.
Imam (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn:
"Do not eat it."
And Imam (as) said: "Whoever wounds an animal with a weapon and mentions the name of Allah (swt), the Mighty and Majestic, upon it, and then the game remains for a night or two, and he finds it without a predator having eaten from it, and he knows that his weapon killed it, then he may eat from it, if Allah (swt) wills."

records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.4140 - Imam (as) said regarding a deer that a man hunted, and people started cutting it up while the one who hunted it was trying to prevent them.
Imam (as) said: "There is no prohibition in it, and there is no harm in it."
records.arabicText: 4140 - وَ قَالَ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ : فِي إِيَّلٍ اِصْطَادَهُ رَجُلٌ فَيُقَطِّعُهُ اَلنَّاسُ وَ اَلَّذِي اِصْطَادَهُ يَمْنَعُهُ فَفِيهِ نَهْيٌ فَقَالَ "وَ لَيْسَ فِيهِ نَهْيٌ وَ لَيْسَ بِهِ بَأْسٌ".
records.thaqalaynSanad:
Hadith.4140 - Imam (as) said regarding a deer that a man hunted, and people started cutting it up while the one who hunted it was trying to prevent them.
Imam (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "There is no prohibition in it, and there is no harm in it."

records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.4141 - Aban narrated from Muhammad Al-Halabi who said:
I asked Imam (as) about a man who shoots at game and brings it down, then the people rush to it and cut it up.
Imam (as) said: "Eat it."
records.arabicText: 4141 - وَ رَوَى أَبَانٌ عَنْ مُحَمَّدٍ اَلْحَلَبِيِّ قَالَ : سَأَلْتُهُ عَنِ اَلرَّجُلِ يَرْمِي اَلصَّيْدَ فَيَصْرَعُهُ فَيَبْتَدِرُهُ اَلْقَوْمُ فَيُقَطِّعُونَهُ فَقَالَ "كُلْهُ".
records.thaqalaynSanad:
Hadith.4141 - Aban narrated from Muhammad Al-Halabi who said:
I asked Imam (as) about a man who shoots at game and brings it down, then the people rush to it and cut it up.
Imam (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "Eat it."

records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.4142 - Al-Mufaddal ibn Salih narrated from Aban ibn Taghlib who said:
I heard Abu Abdillah (as) saying: "My father (as) used to issue legal rulings during the time of Banu Umayyah that what was killed by the hawk and the falcon was lawful, and he would do so out of precaution (Taqiyyah) from them.
But I do not fear them, and what is killed by the hawk and the falcon is unlawful."
records.arabicText: 4142 - وَ رَوَى اَلْمُفَضَّلُ بْنُ صَالِحٍ عَنْ أَبَانِ بْنِ تَغْلِبَ قَالَ سَمِعْتُ أَبَا عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ يَقُولُ : "كَانَ أَبِي عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ يُفْتِي فِي زَمَنِ بَنِي أُمَيَّةَ "أَنَّ مَا قَتَلَ اَلْبَازُ وَ اَلصَّقْرُ فَهُوَ حَلاَلٌ" وَ كَانَ يَتَّقِيهِمْ وَ أَنَا لاَ أَتَّقِيهِمْ وَ هُوَ حَرَامٌ مَا قَتَلَ اَلْبَازُ وَ اَلصَّقْرُ".
records.thaqalaynSanad:
Hadith.4142 - Al-Mufaddal ibn Salih narrated from Aban ibn Taghlib who said:
I heard Abu Abdillah (as) saying:
records.thaqalaynMatn:
"My father (as) used to issue legal rulings during the time of Banu Umayyah that what was killed by the hawk and the falcon was lawful, and he would do so out of precaution (Taqiyyah) from them.
But I do not fear them, and what is killed by the hawk and the falcon is unlawful."

records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.4143 - Abu Basir narrated from Abu Abdillah (as) that Imam (as) said:
"If you release a hawk, a falcon, or an eagle and it kills, do not eat it until you properly slaughter it."
records.arabicText: 4143 - وَ رَوَى أَبُو بَصِيرٍ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ أَنَّهُ قَالَ : "إِنْ أَرْسَلْتَ بَازاً أَوْ صَقْراً أَوْ عُقَاباً فَقَتَلَ فَلاَ تَأْكُلْ حَتَّى تُذَكِّيَهُ".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.4143 - Abu Basir narrated from Abu Abdillah (as) that Imam (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "If you release a hawk, a falcon, or an eagle and it kills, do not eat it until you properly slaughter it."

records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText: Hadith.4144 - Imam (as) said: "If you send your hunting dog after a game and you catch up with it but do not have a blade to slaughter it with, then let the dog kill it, and you may eat from it."
records.arabicText: 4144 - وَ قَالَ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ : "إِنْ أَرْسَلْتَ كَلْبَكَ عَلَى صَيْدٍ فَأَدْرَكْتَهُ وَ لَمْ تَكُنْ مَعَكَ حَدِيدَةٌ تَذْبَحُهُ بِهَا فَدَعِ اَلْكَلْبَ يَقْتُلُهُ ثُمَّ كُلْ مِنْهُ".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.4144 - Imam (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "If you send your hunting dog after a game and you catch up with it but do not have a blade to slaughter it with, then let the dog kill it, and you may eat from it."

records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText: Hadith.4145 - Commander of the Faithful (as) forbade hunting pigeons in cities.
records.arabicText: 4145 - وَ نَهَى أَمِيرُ اَلْمُؤْمِنِينَ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ عَنْ صَيْدِ اَلْحَمَامِ بِالْأَمْصَارِ.

records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.4146 - Ibn Abi Umayr narrated from Ali ibn Ri'ab, from Zurara ibn Ayan, that he said:
By Allah (swt), I have never seen anyone like Abu Jafar Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir (as).
I asked him and said: "May Allah (swt) rectify your affairs, what birds are permissible to eat?"
Imam (as) said: "Eat whatever flaps its wings, and do not eat what glides."
I said: "What about eggs found in thickets?"
Imam (as) said: "If both ends are equal, then do not eat it, but if the two ends are different, then eat it."
I asked: "What about water birds?"
Imam (as) said: "Eat whatever has a crop, and do not eat what does not have a crop."
In another narration, Imam (as) said: "If the bird glides and flaps, but its flapping is more than its gliding, then it may be eaten; but if its gliding is more than its flapping, then it should not be eaten. From water birds, what has a crop or a spur may be eaten, and what lacks them should not be eaten."
records.arabicText:
4146 - وَ رَوَى اِبْنُ أَبِي عُمَيْرٍ عَنْ عَلِيِّ بْنِ رِئَابٍ عَنْ زُرَارَةَ بْنِ أَعْيَنَ أَنَّهُ قَالَ: وَ اَللَّهِ مَا رَأَيْتُ مِثْلَ أَبِي جَعْفَرٍ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ قَطُّ سَأَلْتُهُ فَقُلْتُ أَصْلَحَكَ اَللَّهُ مَا يُؤْكَلُ مِنَ اَلطَّيْرِ فَقَالَ "كُلْ مَا دَفَّ وَ لاَ تَأْكُلْ مَا صَفَّ" قَالَ قُلْتُ اَلْبَيْضُ فِي اَلْآجَامِ قَالَ "كُلُّ مَا اِسْتَوَى طَرَفَاهُ فَلاَ تَأْكُلْ وَ كُلُّ مَا اِخْتَلَفَ طَرَفَاهُ فَكُلْ" قُلْتُ فَطَيْرُ اَلْمَاءِ قَالَ "كُلُّ مَا كَانَتْ لَهُ قَانِصَةٌ فَكُلْ وَ مَا لَمْ تَكُنْ لَهُ قَانِصَةٌ فَلاَ تَأْكُلْ ".
وَ فِي حَدِيثٍ آخَرَ: "إِنْ كَانَ اَلطَّيْرُ يَصُفُّ وَ يَدُفُّ فَكَانَ دَفِيفُهُ أَكْثَرَ مِنْ صَفِيفِهِ أُكِلَ وَ إِنْ كَانَ صَفِيفُهُ أَكْثَرَ مِنْ دَفِيفِهِ فَلَمْ يُؤْكَلْ وَ يُؤْكَلُ مِنْ طَيْرِ اَلْمَاءِ مَا كَانَتْ لَهُ قَانِصَةٌ أَوْ صِيصِيَةٌ وَ لاَ يُؤْكَلُ مَا لَيْسَتْ لَهُ قَانِصَةٌ أَوْ صِيصِيَةٌ".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.4146 - Ibn Abi Umayr narrated from Ali ibn Ri'ab, from Zurara ibn Ayan, that he said:
records.thaqalaynMatn:
By Allah (swt), I have never seen anyone like Abu Jafar Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir (as).
I asked him and said: "May Allah (swt) rectify your affairs, what birds are permissible to eat?"
Imam (as) said: "Eat whatever flaps its wings, and do not eat what glides."
I said: "What about eggs found in thickets?"
Imam (as) said: "If both ends are equal, then do not eat it, but if the two ends are different, then eat it."
I asked: "What about water birds?"
Imam (as) said: "Eat whatever has a crop, and do not eat what does not have a crop."
In another narration, Imam (as) said: "If the bird glides and flaps, but its flapping is more than its gliding, then it may be eaten; but if its gliding is more than its flapping, then it should not be eaten. From water birds, what has a crop or a spur may be eaten, and what lacks them should not be eaten."

records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText: Hadith.4147 - The Messenger of Allah (swt) (peace and blessings be upon him and his family) said: "Every predatory animal with fangs and every bird with talons is unlawful."
records.arabicText: 4147 - وَ قَالَ رَسُولُ اَللَّهِ صَلَّى اَللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَ آلِهِ : "كُلُّ ذِي نَابٍ مِنَ اَلسِّبَاعِ وَ مِخْلَبٍ مِنَ اَلطَّيْرِ حَرَامٌ".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.4147 - The Messenger of Allah (swt) (peace and blessings be upon him and his family) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "Every predatory animal with fangs and every bird with talons is unlawful."

records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.4148 - Safwan ibn Yahya narrated from Muhammad ibn al-Harith who said: I asked Abu al-Hasan (as) about water birds that eat fish, whether they are permissible to eat.
He (as) said: "There is no harm in it, eat them."
records.arabicText: 4148 - وَ رَوَى صَفْوَانُ بْنُ يَحْيَى عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ اَلْحَارِثِ قَالَ : سَأَلْتُ أَبَا اَلْحَسَنِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ عَنْ طَيْرِ اَلْمَاءِ مِمَّا يَأْكُلُ اَلسَّمَكَ مِنْهُ يَحِلُّ قَالَ "لاَ بَأْسَ بِهِ كُلْهُ".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.4148 - Safwan ibn Yahya narrated from Muhammad ibn al-Harith who said: I asked Abu al-Hasan (as) about water birds that eat fish, whether they are permissible to eat.
records.thaqalaynMatn: He (as) said: "There is no harm in it, eat them."

records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.4149 - Kirdin al-Mismai asked Abu Abdillah (as) about the bustard bird.
Imam (as) said: "I wish I had some of it so that I could eat until I am full."
records.arabicText: 4149 - وَ سَأَلَ كِرْدِينٌ اَلْمِسْمَعِيُّ أَبَا عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ : عَنِ اَلْحُبَارَى فَقَالَ "لَوَدِدْتُ أَنَّ عِنْدِي مِنْهُ فَآكُلُ حَتَّى أَمْتَلِئَ".

records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.4150 - Zakariyya ibn Adam asked Abu al-Hasan (as) about water birds.
Imam (as) said: "If they pick up things other than filth, then there is no harm in eating them."
records.arabicText: 4150 - وَ سَأَلَ زَكَرِيَّا بْنُ آدَمَ أَبَا اَلْحَسَنِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ : عَنْ دَجَاجِ اَلْمَاءِ فَقَالَ "إِذَا كَانَ يَلْتَقِطُ غَيْرَ اَلْعَذِرَةِ فَلاَ بَأْسَ بِهِ ".
records.thaqalaynSanad:
Hadith.4150 - Zakariyya ibn Adam asked Abu al-Hasan (as) about water birds.
Imam (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "If they pick up things other than filth, then there is no harm in eating them."

records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.4151 - Abdullah ibn Sinan asked Abu Abdillah (as) about the eggs of water birds.
Imam (as) said: "Whatever resembles the eggs of chickens in shape, then eat it."
records.arabicText: 4151 - وَ سَأَلَ عَبْدُ اَللَّهِ بْنُ سِنَانٍ أَبَا عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ : عَنْ بَيْضِ طَيْرِ اَلْمَاءِ فَقَالَ "مَا كَانَ مِنْهُ مِثْلَ بَيْضِ اَلدَّجَاجِ يَعْنِي عَلَى خِلْقَتِهِ فَكُلْ".
records.thaqalaynSanad:
Hadith.4151 - Abdullah ibn Sinan asked Abu Abdillah (as) about the eggs of water birds.
Imam (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "Whatever resembles the eggs of chickens in shape, then eat it."

records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.4152 - Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) said:
"Eat from the fish that has scales, and do not eat from it that which has no scales."
records.arabicText: 4152 - وَ قَالَ اَلصَّادِقُ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ : "كُلْ مِنَ اَلسَّمَكِ مَا كَانَ لَهُ فُلُوسٌ وَ لاَ تَأْكُلْ مِنْهُ مَا لَيْسَ لَهُ فَلْسٌ ".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.4152 - Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "Eat from the fish that has scales, and do not eat from it that which has no scales."

records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.4153 - Hammad narrated from Abu Ayyub that he asked Abu Abdillah (as) about a man who caught a fish, tied it with a string, and released it into the water, and then it died.
Imam (as) said: "It should not be eaten."
records.arabicText: 4153 - وَ رَوَى حَمَّادٌ عَنْ أَبِي أَيُّوبَ : أَنَّهُ سَأَلَ أَبَا عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ عَنْ رَجُلٍ اِصْطَادَ سَمَكَةً فَرَبَطَهَا بِخَيْطٍ وَ أَرْسَلَهَا فِي اَلْمَاءِ فَمَاتَتْ أَ تُؤْكَلُ قَالَ "لاَ".
records.thaqalaynSanad:
Hadith.4153 - Hammad narrated from Abu Ayyub that he asked Abu Abdillah (as) about a man who caught a fish, tied it with a string, and released it into the water, and then it died.
Imam (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "It should not be eaten."

records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.4154 - Abd al-Rahman ibn Sayabah asked him about fish that are caught, placed in something, and then returned to the water where they die.
Imam (as) said: "Do not eat it, because it died in what is its source of life."
records.arabicText: 4154 - وَ سَأَلَهُ عَبْدُ اَلرَّحْمَنِ بْنُ سَيَابَةَ : عَنِ اَلسَّمَكِ يُصَادُ ثُمَّ يُجْعَلُ فِي شَيْءٍ ثُمَّ يُعَادُ فِي اَلْمَاءِ فَيَمُوتُ فِيهِ فَقَالَ "لاَ تَأْكُلْ لِأَنَّهُ مَاتَ فِي اَلَّذِي فِيهِ حَيَاتُهُ ".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.4154 - Abd al-Rahman ibn Sayabah asked him about fish that are caught, placed in something, and then returned to the water where they die.
records.thaqalaynMatn: Imam (as) said: "Do not eat it, because it died in what is its source of life."

records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.4155 - Aban narrated from Zurara who said: I asked him about a fish that leaps out of the water, falls onto dry land, and struggles until it dies.
I said: "Can I eat it?"
Imam (as) said: "Yes."
records.arabicText: 4155 - وَ رَوَى أَبَانٌ عَنْ زُرَارَةَ قَالَ : قُلْتُ لَهُ سَمَكَةٌ اِرْتَفَعَتْ فَوَقَعَتْ عَلَى اَلْجَدَدِ فَاضْطَرَبَتْ حَتَّى مَاتَتْ آكُلُهَا قَالَ "نَعَمْ".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.4155 - Aban narrated from Zurara who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn:
I asked him about a fish that leaps out of the water, falls onto dry land, and struggles until it dies.
I said: "Can I eat it?"
Imam (as) said: "Yes."

records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.4156 - Al-Qasim ibn Burayd narrated from Muhammad ibn Muslim, from Abu Jafar Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir (as), regarding a man who sets up a net in the water, then returns to his house and leaves it set up.
Later, he comes back and finds that fish have been caught in it and have died.
Imam (as) said: "What his hand has done is permissible, so there is no harm in eating what was caught in it."
records.arabicText: 4156 - وَ رَوَى اَلْقَاسِمُ بْنُ بُرَيْدٍ عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ مُسْلِمٍ عَنْ أَبِي جَعْفَرٍ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ : فِي رَجُلٍ نَصَبَ شَبَكَةً فِي اَلْمَاءِ ثُمَّ رَجَعَ إِلَى بَيْتِهِ وَ تَرَكَهَا مَنْصُوبَةً ثُمَّ أَتَاهَا بَعْدَ ذَلِكَ وَ قَدْ وَقَعَ فِيهَا سَمَكٌ فَمُوِّتْنَ فَقَالَ "مَا عَمِلَتْ يَدُهُ فَلاَ بَأْسَ بِأَكْلِ مَا وَقَعَ فِيهِ".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.4156 - Al-Qasim ibn Burayd narrated from Muhammad ibn Muslim, from Abu Jafar Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir (as),
records.thaqalaynMatn:
regarding a man who sets up a net in the water, then returns to his house and leaves it set up.
Later, he comes back and finds that fish have been caught in it and have died.
Imam (as) said: "What his hand has done is permissible, so there is no harm in eating what was caught in it."

records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.4157 - Abu al-Sabbah al-Kinani asked Abu Abdillah (as) about fish caught by the Magians.
Imam (as) said: "There is no harm in it; the catching of fish is merely taking them."
records.arabicText: 4157 - وَ سَأَلَ أَبُو اَلصَّبَّاحِ اَلْكِنَانِيُّ أَبَا عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ : عَنِ اَلْحِيتَانِ يَصِيدُهَا اَلْمَجُوسُ قَالَ "لاَ بَأْسَ بِهَا إِنَّمَا صَيْدُ اَلْحِيتَانِ أَخْذُهَا".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.4157 - Abu al-Sabbah al-Kinani asked Abu Abdillah (as) about fish caught by the Magians.
records.thaqalaynMatn: Imam (as) said: "There is no harm in it; the catching of fish is merely taking them."

records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.4158 - In the narration of Abdullah ibn Sinan from Abu Abdillah (as), Imam (as) said:
"There is no harm in pickled foods of the Magians, and there is no harm in their catching of fish."
records.arabicText: 4158 - وَ فِي رِوَايَةِ عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ بْنِ سِنَانٍ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ قَالَ : "لاَ بَأْسَ بِكَوَامِيخِ اَلْمَجُوسِ وَ لاَ بَأْسَ بِصَيْدِهِمُ اَلسَّمَكَ".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.4158 - In the narration of Abdullah ibn Sinan from Abu Abdillah (as), Imam (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "There is no harm in pickled foods of the Magians, and there is no harm in their catching of fish."

records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.4159 - He said: I asked him about an enclosure made of reeds placed in the water for fish, and the fish enter it, and some of them die inside it.
Imam (as) said: "There is no harm in it."
records.arabicText: 4159 - قَالَ : وَ سَأَلْتُهُ عَنِ اَلْحَظِيرَةِ مِنَ اَلْقَصَبِ تُجْعَلُ لِلْحِيتَانِ فِي اَلْمَاءِ فَيَدْخُلُهَا اَلْحِيتَانُ فَيَمُوتُ بَعْضُهَا فِيهَا قَالَ "لاَ بَأْسَ ".
records.thaqalaynSanad:
Hadith.4159 - He said: I asked him about an enclosure made of reeds placed in the water for fish, and the fish enter it, and some of them die inside it.
Imam (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "There is no harm in it."

records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.4160 - Al-Halabi asked him about catching fish without mentioning the name of Allah (swt) over it.
Imam (as) said: "There is no harm in it."
records.arabicText: 4160 - وَ سَأَلَهُ اَلْحَلَبِيُّ : عَنْ صَيْدِ اَلْحِيتَانِ وَ إِنْ لَمْ يُسَمَّ فَقَالَ "لاَ بَأْسَ بِهِ".
records.thaqalaynSanad:
Hadith.4160 - Al-Halabi asked him about catching fish without mentioning the name of Allah (swt) over it.
Imam (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "There is no harm in it."

records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.4161 - Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) said:
"Do not eat the catfish, nor the eel, nor the zabir, nor the tafiy, which is that which dies in the water and floats on the surface of the water."
records.arabicText: 4161 - وَ قَالَ اَلصَّادِقُ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ : "لاَ تَأْكُلِ اَلْجِرِّيَّ وَ لاَ اَلْمَارْمَاهِيَ وَ لاَ اَلزِّمِّيرَ وَ لاَ اَلطَّافِيَ وَ هُوَ اَلَّذِي يَمُوتُ فِي اَلْمَاءِ فَيَطْفُو عَلَى رَأْسِ اَلْمَاءِ".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.4161 - Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "Do not eat the catfish, nor the eel, nor the zabir, nor the tafiy, which is that which dies in the water and floats on the surface of the water."

records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.4162 - It has been narrated: "If someone finds a fish and does not know whether it is permissible to eat or not, then its tail should be split at the base. If the color turns green, it is not permissible to eat; but if it turns red, then it is permissible to eat.
And if a snake swallows a fish and then spits it out while it is still alive and wriggling, if its scales have peeled off, it should not be eaten; but if its scales have not peeled off, it may be eaten."
records.arabicText: 4162 - وَ رُوِيَ : "فِيمَنْ وَجَدَ سَمَكاً وَ لَمْ يَعْلَمْ أَنَّهُ مِمَّا يُؤْكَلُ أَوْ لاَ فَإِنَّهُ يُشَقُّ أَصْلُ ذَنَبِهِ فَإِنْ ضَرَبَ إِلَى اَلْخُضْرَةِ فَهُوَ مِمَّا لاَ يُؤْكَلُ وَ إِنْ ضَرَبَ إِلَى اَلْحُمْرَةِ فَهُوَ مِمَّا يُؤْكَلُ وَ إِنِ اِبْتَلَعَتْ حَيَّةٌ سَمَكَةً ثُمَّ رَمَتْ بِهَا وَ هِيَ حَيَّةٌ تَضْطَرِبُ فَإِنْ كَانَ فُلُوسُهَا قَدْ تَسَلَّخَتْ لَمْ تُؤْكَلْ وَ إِنْ لَمْ يَكُنْ فُلُوسُهَا تَسَلَّخَتْ أُكِلَتْ ".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.4162 - It has been narrated:
records.thaqalaynMatn:
"If someone finds a fish and does not know whether it is permissible to eat or not, then its tail should be split at the base. If the color turns green, it is not permissible to eat; but if it turns red, then it is permissible to eat.
And if a snake swallows a fish and then spits it out while it is still alive and wriggling, if its scales have peeled off, it should not be eaten; but if its scales have not peeled off, it may be eaten."

records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.4163 - Safwan ibn Yahya narrated from Abd al-Rahman ibn al-Hajjaj who said: I asked Abu Ibrahim (Imam Musa ibn Jafar Al-Kadhim (as)) about using a flint, a reed, or a piece of wood to slaughter an animal if one does not have a knife.
Imam (as) said: "If it cuts the veins, then there is no harm in it."
records.arabicText: 4163 - وَ رَوَى صَفْوَانُ بْنُ يَحْيَى عَنْ عَبْدِ اَلرَّحْمَنِ بْنِ اَلْحَجَّاجِ قَالَ : سَأَلْتُ أَبَا إِبْرَاهِيمَ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ عَنِ اَلْمَرْوَةِ وَ اَلْقَصَبَةِ وَ اَلْعُودِ يَذْبَحُ بِهِنَّ اَلْإِنْسَانُ إِذَا لَمْ يَجِدْ سِكِّيناً فَقَالَ "إِذَا فَرَى اَلْأَوْدَاجَ فَلاَ بَأْسَ بِذَلِكَ ".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.4163 - Safwan ibn Yahya narrated from Abd al-Rahman ibn al-Hajjaj who said: I asked Abu Ibrahim (Imam Musa ibn Jafar Al-Kadhim (as)) about using a flint, a reed, or a piece of wood to slaughter an animal if one does not have a knife.
records.thaqalaynMatn: Imam (as) said: "If it cuts the veins, then there is no harm in it."
